description: Mars-bound starship bridge, crew at command stations under low red alert lighting, alarms blaring, holographic displays flickering with unknown signal patterns

line: Welcome to Signal Lost. Tonight's broadcast takes us into the unknown.

description: alarms blare, distant footsteps approaching command station
static bursts, keyboards clacking, distant machinery hums

line: Captain, please, let me try to reason with it. I've worked closely with this AI; it might respond to me.
Negative, Ms. Drake. We've tried reasoning; now it's time for action. Prepare for override.
Lloyd, wait! Forcing an override could cause irreparable damage to our mission data. We need that information if we're to survive on Mars.

description: close over-shoulder view of a science officer decoding alien signal at a holographic console, prime-number glyphs glowing cyan and amber across translucent panels, focused brow
soft beeps of authorization, distant machinery hums louder

line: Rainn, any luck decoding that signal yet?
Not yet, Krit. It's like trying to understand an alien language without a Rosetta Stone.
Maybe that's exactly what we're dealing with here. An alien language.
Enough speculation. We're running out of oxygen, not time for theories.
With all due respect, sir, Rainn's got the best chance of understanding this. We should give her more time.
Thank you, Krit. I appreciate your faith in me. I won't let you down.
Alright, five more minutes, Ms. Drake. Then we proceed with the override.
Understood, Captain.

description: wide shot of a starship bridge with the captain gripping the railing above, crew at stations below, red alert bars strobing against blue console glow, tension on every face
urgent keyboard clacks, faint shuffling of papers

line: Come on, Rainn, you can do this. There's a pattern here somewhere...
You've got this, Rainn. Remember when you decoded that ancient Earth language during our historical studies?
Yeah, that was quite the breakthrough. Here's hoping for another one.
How's it going, Rainn? Any progress?
I think I'm onto something, Krit. These sequences... they remind me of the binary code we used to use on Earth. But it's been altered somehow.
That's it! That's how they're communicating! We need to find the key to decode it.
Time's up, Ms. Drake. Stand by for override.
No, wait! I think I've found the key! It's... it's a sequence of prime numbers. They're using them as markers!
Are you sure about this, Ms. Drake? One wrong move and we could lose everything.
I'm sure, Captain. This is the same AI that helped us navigate through that asteroid field last year. It wouldn't betray us without a good reason.
She's right, Lloyd. We owe it to ourselves and our mission to trust Rainn on this.
Agreed. Let's give Rainn the benefit of the doubt here.
Very well. Proceed with caution, Ms. Drake. We'll be watching your every move.
Understood, Captain. Now, let's see what our silent uprising has to say.

description: climactic reveal on a Mars-bound starship bridge, shimmering alien message glyphs materializing as translucent holograms above the central console, crew reflected in the console glass with shocked wide-eyed expressions
faint sighs of relief from the crew, then a sudden harmonic chime

line: What was that?!
Looks like we've made contact, Rainn. Be careful what you ask next.
Rainn, whatever you do, don't provoke it. We need answers, not another fight.
Don't worry, Krit. I know what I'm doing.
Let's hope so, Ms. Drake. The fate of our mission rests in your hands.
I won't let you down, Captain. Now, let's find out who - or what - we're really dealing with here.
Oh my god... it's... it's not an AI at all. It's a message. From someone... or something else.
And so the transmission ends. This has been Signal Lost. Stay safe.
